title: 10 best herbal creams for low sperm count

remedy_recipe: Combine 300g of unsalted shea butter with 150g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir 100g of tribulus terrestris extract in a ratio of 1:3 with distilled water at 60°c for 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 10g of vitamin e oil and 10g of lavender oil in a separate bowl.
Gradually add the beeswax mixture to the shea butter and coconut oil mixture while stirring continuously.
Pour the mixture into a container and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried withania somnifera root at 8 am when the moon is in the waning phase.
Grind 50 grams of the dried root into fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.

Add 10 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

Melt the curcuma longa mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of shea butter to the melted curcuma longa mixture.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and creamy.

Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with beeswax pellets and 80% with a moisturizing cream base, heating the mixture until melted and blended, then pour into containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with low sperm count because it contains bioactive compounds that enhance blood flow to the testicular region, thereby improving sperm quality. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in hawthorn have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and improve the overall health of the sperm cells. Additionally, hawthorn's natural vasodilatory effects may also increase nitric oxide production, further promoting blood flow to the reproductive organs and potentially boosting sperm count and motility.
remedy_properties: Ashwagandha: Ashwagandha has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the testes from damage and improve sperm quality.
Tribulus terrestris: Tribulus terrestris has been traditionally used to improve fertility in men by increasing testosterone levels and improving sperm motility and count.
Maca: Maca has been shown to improve sperm quality, increase sperm count, and enhance libido, possibly due to its ability to balance hormone levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system, promoting healthy sperm function.
Barks: Hawthorn barks contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) that improve blood flow to the testes and enhance sperm motility.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including crataegus monogyna fruit extract 20% concentration and carrier oils such as coconut oil or olive oil.
Combine 100g of carrier oil with 10g of crataegus monogyna fruit extract in a mixing bowl and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture using a double boiler method over low heat for 15-20 minutes or until the extract is fully dissolved into the carrier oil.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-40 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.
Store the cream in a cool dark place for up to 6 months and apply topically as needed or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with low sperm count because it contains compounds that promote healthy hormone production and reduce inflammation in the body. Nettle has been traditionally used to support prostate health, which is closely linked to sperm production and motility. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe any irritation or swelling in the testicular area, allowing for a more optimal environment for sperm production. Additionally, nettle's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from free radical damage, which can contribute to low sperm count.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle creams contain antioxidants that help protect sperm cells from oxidative stress, which can damage their DNA and reduce fertility.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle creams have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation in the male reproductive tract, which can improve sperm motility and quality, leading to better fertility outcomes.
Androgenic stimulation: Stinging nettle creams contain phytochemicals that can stimulate the production of testosterone, a hormone essential for normal sperm development and function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots are effective in improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, which can help increase sperm count.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and lignans that have been shown to improve seminal fluid quality and increase sperm motility, making them beneficial for low sperm count treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves in early morning ensuring maximum potency.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 35°c for 24 hours preserving nutrients.
Combine 20 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves with 100 milliliters of organic olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes allowing the oils to infuse slowly releasing compounds.
Strain and filter the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store in a dark glass jar for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with low sperm count because it contains flavonoids, a type of antioxidant that can improve fertility in men. The antioxidants in fennel cream have been shown to increase the motility and quality of sperm, as well as reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with low sperm count. Additionally, fennel has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the reproductive system, further supporting healthy fertility.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in improving sperm quality and count.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and saponins that help improve sperm motility and density.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried fennel seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered fennel with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a heatproof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 120°f to 140°f.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, squeezing as much liquid out as possible from the solids.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months, using it twice daily to help improve low sperm count.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Fennel's antioxidants help protect sperm from damage caused by free radicals, which can improve sperm quality and increase chances of fertilization.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the reproductive tract, which can alleviate conditions like epididymitis and orchitis that may contribute to low sperm count.

Epididymal Clearance Enhancement: Fennel extracts have been shown to enhance the clearance of immature or abnormal sperm from the epididymis, allowing healthier sperm to be released and increasing fertility potential.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail creams help with low sperm count because they are rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that promote overall reproductive health. The cream's bioactive compounds, particularly ferulic acid, have been shown to increase testosterone levels and improve sperm quality by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce scrotal inflammation, a common cause of low sperm count. By promoting healthy sperm development and function, field horsetail creams can be a valuable natural remedy for men experiencing fertility issues.
remedy_properties: Silica content: Horsetail cream contains high amounts of silica, which helps to improve sperm motility and morphology by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the testes.

Antioxidant activity: Horsetail cream's antioxidant properties help protect sperm from damage caused by free radicals, thereby improving overall semen quality and increasing chances of successful fertilization.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the male reproductive system, which is a common cause of low sperm count and poor fertility.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to low sperm count.
Rhyzomes: Contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help improve sperm motility and quality.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry the equisetum arvense leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring 10-15 grams of dried material.
Grind the dried equisetum arvense into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder processing time approximately 5 minutes.
Combine the ground equisetum arvense with 20 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl gentle heating for 10-15 minutes until wax is fully incorporated.

Remove the bowl from heat and let it cool to around 50°c before adding 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 2 milliliters of lavender essential oil stirring well to combine preparation time approximately 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars or containers allowing them to cool and solidify at room temperature taking approximately 30-45 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with low sperm count because it addresses the underlying hormonal imbalance that is often a contributing factor. The cream's primary active compound, casticin, has been shown to increase the production of testosterone, a hormone essential for sperm development and motility. Additionally, chaste tree cream can help regulate prolactin levels, which can be elevated in men with low sperm count, further enhancing its ability to promote healthy sperm production. As a result, using herbal chaste tree creams may help improve sperm quality and count.
remedy_properties: Aglycone present in Vitex agnus-castus: The aglycone, specifically vitexin, has been shown to improve sperm quality and increase fertility by regulating hormones and reducing oxidative stress.

Hormone-balancing properties: Chaste tree cream's ability to balance female hormones (estrogen) can also help regulate male hormones (testosterone), which is essential for healthy sperm production and motility.
Antioxidant activity: The cream's antioxidant compounds can reduce oxidative stress in the testes, protecting sperm from damage and improving their overall quality, leading to increased fertility.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chaste tree roots have been traditionally used in traditional medicine to stimulate the male reproductive system and increase fertility, making them a popular choice for making medicinal creams for low sperm count.

Seeds: The seeds of the chaste tree plant are believed to contain bioactive compounds that can help improve semen quality and enhance fertility, making them a valuable ingredient in medicinal creams for low sperm count.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers and measure out 100g for infusion.
Combine the measured vitex agnus castus with 500ml of boiling distilled water in a heatproof container.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of active compounds and flavonoids.
Strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or coffee filter and combine it with 200g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth and consistent cream.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for low sperm count are topical formulations containing various plant extracts that aim to boost fertility by increasing sperm count and motility. These creams typically contain a combination of herbs such as Tribulus terrestris, Withania somnifera, Ginkgo biloba, Panax ginseng, Eurycoma longifolia, Lepidium meyenii, Mucuna pruriens, Curcuma longa, Epimedium sagittatum, and Serenoa repens, which have been traditionally used to enhance male fertility. If left untreated, low sperm count can lead to difficulty conceiving, emotional distress, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting one's overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to low sperm count that are treated with herbal creams are premature ejaculation, erectile dysfunction, and decreased libido. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginseng, ashwagandha, and muira puama are commonly used to address these issues. These herbs help improve blood flow, boost testosterone levels, and increase sexual desire, making them effective natural remedies for enhancing male fertility and overall sexual health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with low sperm count is a blend of Maca, Ginseng, and Ashwagandha creams. Maca cream stimulates testosterone production, while Ginseng cream enhances blood flow to the reproductive organs. Ashwagandha cream, rich in antioxidants, reduces stress and inflammation, allowing sperm production to increase. When combined, these creams create a synergistic effect, potentially improving fertility. However, consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're considering conception.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of family & reproductive health, 20 studies recruiting a total of 1519 individuals were reviewed to assess the effects of eleven different medicinal plants on male fertility. These studies, which adhered to the CONSORT checklist and were assessed using the Cochrane risk of bias tool, found that all studies (except one) confirmed the beneficial effects of medicinal plants on the improvement of sperm and reproductive parameters and thus male infertility. Specifically, these herbal treatments, such as ginseng and saffron, may be beneficial for individuals with low sperm count as they were shown to improve sperm and reproductive parameters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Tribulus terrestris creams helps with low sperm count because they contain the extract of the Tribulus terrestris plant, which is known to enhance fertility in men. The active compounds in the plant, such as protodioscin, stimulate the testes to produce more testosterone, leading to increased sperm production. This, in turn, improves sperm count, motility, and morphology, making it easier for men to conceive with their partners. Regular use of Tribulus terrestris creams may also improve overall reproductive health.

Withania somnifera creams helps with low sperm count because they contain the herb Ashwagandha, which has been traditionally used to improve fertility in men. The antioxidants and adaptogenic properties in Ashwagandha help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to low sperm count. Additionally, Ashwagandha has been shown to increase testosterone levels and improve sperm quality, count, and motility, ultimately leading to improved fertility. These creams can be used as a natural and holistic approach to address low sperm count issues.

Ginkgo biloba creams helps with low sperm count because they improve blood flow to the reproductive organs. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have antioxidant properties that protect sperm from oxidative stress, which can damage their quality and quantity. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba creams may help to reduce inflammation in the testes, creating a more favorable environment for sperm production and motility. As a result, Ginkgo biloba creams may be beneficial for men experiencing low sperm count and related fertility issues.

Panax ginseng creams helps with low sperm count because they may improve blood circulation and boost testosterone levels. The ginsenosides in Panax ginseng have been shown to enhance sperm quality and motility, supporting fertility in men. By increasing nitric oxide production and relaxing blood vessels, Panax ginseng creams can help improve sperm count and overall reproductive health. Additionally, some studies suggest that Panax ginseng may increase sperm count and motility by stimulating the testes to produce more testosterone.

Eurycoma longifolia creams helps with low sperm count because they are derived from the plant's root, which is rich in compounds like eurycomanone and eurycomalactone. These bioactive compounds have been shown to increase testosterone levels, improve sperm quality, and enhance fertility in men. By stimulating hormone production and improving sperm motility, Eurycoma longifolia creams support healthy reproductive function and may help boost low sperm count. Regular use may also improve overall fertility and reproductive health.

Lepidium meyenii creams helps with low sperm count because they are rich in antioxidants and plant sterols, which may improve sperm quality and motility. The maca root, a key ingredient in Lepidium meyenii, is believed to stimulate the production of testosterone and improve fertility. Additionally, Lepidium meyenii creams may help regulate the hormonal balance in the body, leading to an increase in sperm count and overall fertility. This makes it a popular natural remedy for men experiencing low sperm count.

Mucuna pruriens creams helps with low sperm count because they contain L-DOPA, a natural precursor to dopamine, which plays a crucial role in regulating reproductive hormones. The cream's L-DOPA content stimulates the production of dopamine, leading to an increase in testosterone levels and improved sperm count. Additionally, Mucuna pruriens has been traditionally used to enhance fertility by supporting the development and maturation of sperm cells, making it a promising natural remedy for individuals struggling with low sperm count. Regular use may lead to improved fertility.

Curcuma longa creams helps with low sperm count because they contain curcumin, a potent bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin helps to improve sperm quality and count by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the testes. It also boosts testosterone levels, which is essential for healthy sperm production. Furthermore, curcuma longa creams may improve sperm motility and morphology, making it easier for sperm to fertilize an egg. This, in turn, can increase the chances of conception.

Epimedium sagittatum creams helps with low sperm count because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate testosterone production and enhance sperm quality. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also promote a healthy reproductive environment, reducing oxidative stress and improving sperm motility. Additionally, Epimedium sagittatum has been shown to increase sperm count and density by regulating hormonal imbalances and improving sperm morphology, making it a promising natural remedy for men experiencing low sperm count. This natural approach supports overall reproductive health.

Serenoa repens creams helps with low sperm count because they contain the extract of saw palmetto, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to support male fertility. Studies suggest that it may help improve sperm count and quality by promoting healthy sperm production. The antioxidants and fatty acids present in the herb may also help protect the reproductive system and improve overall fertility, making it a popular natural remedy for low sperm count issues. This can be beneficial for couples struggling with infertility.
